commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P1] This needs contributor or maintainer action because the repair is narrow, but the current external PR also needs updated real behavior proof that automation cannot supply for the contributor.; Needs attention: The diff changes a credential redaction boundary and can leave app-password-shaped values visible for application-password wording in generic fields.; Review finding: [P1] Keep application-password cont... (sha=afdfeaa120d794edc990fdf1e8768674b0c7959e)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds an Apple/iCloud context gate for app-specific-password redaction in generic structured fields and adds regression tests for preserving kebab-case identifiers while keeping dedicated Apple/iCloud fields masked.

PR surface: Source +11, Tests +32. Total +43 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level. Current main unconditionally applies app-password-shaped redaction to broad generic structured fields, and the persisted transcript caller writes redactSecrets output to disk; this read-only review did not run tests.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] This needs contributor or maintainer action because the repair is narrow, but the current external PR also needs updated real behavior proof that automation cannot supply for the contributor.

target.title: fix: tighten apple app-specific password redaction to avoid false positives on kebab-case identifiers (#94254)
target.body:
## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** `redact.ts` was masking any `aaaa-bbbb-cccc-dddd` pattern in generic content fields (text, content, message, error, errorMessage, detail, details, reason) as an Apple app-specific password, corrupting legitimate kebab-case identifiers like `kube-node-pool-spec`. The fix adds an Apple/iCloud context gate so generic fields only run the app-password check when Apple/iCloud context is present in the value.

**Real environment tested:** macOS 14.6.1, Node 22, openclaw main @ 967fb8d9

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
```bash
node --import tsx -e "
import { redactSecrets, redactSensitiveFieldValue } from './src/logging/redact.ts';

// Without Apple context - preserved
console.log('1:', JSON.stringify(redactSensitiveFieldValue('text', 'open the help-desk-team-page link')));
// With iCloud context - masked
console.log('2:', JSON.stringify(redactSensitiveFieldValue('error', 'iCloud rejected abcd-efgh-ijkl-mnop')));
// Apple-specific field - masked
console.log('3:', JSON.stringify(redactSensitiveFieldValue('app-specific-password', 'abcd-efgh-ijkl-mnop')));
// Nested with iCloud context + kebab-case
console.log('4:', JSON.stringify(redactSecrets({data: {text: 'iCloud rejected abcd-efgh-ijkl-mnop', detail: 'kube-node-pool-spec preserved'}})));
"
```

**Evidence after fix:** Terminal output from the above command:

![terminal output](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/001e0c8a-926e-41d5-96c3-c3c760fb77e3)

```
1: "open the help-desk-team-page link"
2: "iCloud rejected abcd-e…mnop"
3: "abcd-e…mnop"
4: {"data":{"text":"iCloud rejected abcd-e…mnop","detail":"kube-node-pool-spec preserved"}}
```

**Observed result after fix:**
- Generic fields without Apple/iCloud context: kebab-case identifiers are preserved (test 1)
- Generic fields with Apple/iCloud context: Apple passwords are still masked (test 2)
- Apple-specific field keys (app-specific-password, icloud): always masked (test 3)
- Nested structured payloads: context-gated masking works correctly (test 4)
- All 111 existing unit tests pass

**What was not tested:** Integration with Telegram/transcript pipeline. The change only affects redact.ts; transcript persistence and other redaction paths are unchanged.

## Out-of-scope follow-ups
None.
target.author: xydigit-zt
